title: Accessible Puzzle Interface and Multichannel Feedback · Language Clinic: Motion Timing Assistance
summary: Walls, visited cells, current focus, route, and errors are communicated only by color and animation, while the Canvas has no usable alternative. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the motion timing assistance increment in keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Offer pause, instant mode, adjustable speed, reduced motion, hints, route reveal, and difficulty dimensions without penalty.
Provide keyboard navigation, remapping, visible focus, large targets, clear labels, no traps, and announced state changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-accessible-interface-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-accessible-interface-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_accessible_interface_3(essential_states: int = 9, text_equivalents: int = 9, keyboard_actions: int = 9, color_only_states: int = 0, open_barriers: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0:
        return False, text_equivalents, "accessibility-count"
    if text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states:
        return False, text_equivalents, "equivalent-experience"
    if color_only_states > 0:
        return False, color_only_states, "color-only-cue"
    if open_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_barriers, "unclosed-barrier"
    assert text_equivalents == essential_states
    return True, text_equivalents, "multichannel-maze-interface"

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-motion-timing-assistance-b9ul13
# Competency: maze-motion-timing-assistance.
# Case 7789: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def evidence_maze_motion_timing_assistance_b9ul13(essential_states: int = 9, text_equivalents: int = 9, keyboard_actions: int = 9, color_only_states: int = 0, open_barriers: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_b9ul13 = "b9ul13-31lu9b-7789"
    assert evidence_variant_b9ul13
    evidence_axes_b9ul13 = (
        "gridb9ul13",
        "topology7789",
        "search31lu9b",
        "eventb9ul137789",
        "access7789b9ul13",
        "recovery31lu9b7789",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_b9ul13)) == 6
    if essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0:
        return False, text_equivalents, "accessibility-count"
    if text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states:
        return False, text_equivalents, "equivalent-experience"
    if color_only_states > 0:
        return False, color_only_states, "color-only-cue"
    if open_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_barriers, "unclosed-barrier"
    assert text_equivalents == essential_states
    return True, text_equivalents, "multichannel-maze-interface"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Motion Timing Assistance model · Theory Motion Timing…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Motion Timing Assistance: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 7424 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Offer pause, instant mode, adjustable speed, reduced motion, hints, route reveal, and difficulty dimensions without penalty.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Motion Timing Assistance model · Theory Motion Timing….
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Motion Timing Assistance model · Theory Motion Timing…, keep Motion Timing Assistance connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Offer pause, instant mode, adjustable speed, reduced motion, hints, route reveal, and difficulty dimensions without penalty.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Motion Timing Assistance model · Theory Motion Timing…, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Motion Timing Assistance precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Motion Timing Assistance model · Theory Motion Timing…, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 7424 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Offer pause, instant mode, adjustable speed, reduced motion, hints, route reveal, and difficulty dimensions without penalty.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Motion Timing Assistance model · Theory Motion Timing…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Animation speed and maze size are the same difficulty setting.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-motion-timing-assistance-b9ul13.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-motion-timing-assistance-b9ul13(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_motion_timing_assistance_b9ul13\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?color_only_states > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?open_barriers > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
